M-048.NAME: installer-wildcard-tls
M-048.path: install.sh deploy/deploy-on-server.sh nginx docker-compose.yml .env.example scripts/phase35-installer-wildcard-tls-smoke.mjs scripts/phase36-resend-deploy-smoke.mjs scripts/phase37-mtproto-runtime-smoke.mjs scripts/phase38-de-mtproto-edge-smoke.mjs docs/plans/Phase-35.xml docs/plans/Phase-36.xml docs/plans/Phase-37.xml docs/plans/Phase-38.xml
M-048.owns: installer prompts, remote wildcard certificate validation, deploy-time certificate installation, production edge env wiring, Phase-36 Resend email env prompts, Phase-37 MTProto runtime token/env wiring, planned Phase-38 DE-backed edge wiring, and operator-safe TLS reuse.
M-048.purpose: Make fresh deploys accept an operator-provided wildcard certificate instead of reissuing or silently generating production self-signed TLS material.

exports.installer-domain-prompt: Prompt for the public domain with krotpn.xyz as the default.
exports.installer-fullchain-prompt: Prompt for the absolute RU-server fullchain path with /root/krotpn-ssl/fullchain1.pem as the default.
exports.installer-privkey-prompt: Prompt for the absolute RU-server private key path with /root/krotpn-ssl/privkey1.pem as the default.
exports.installer-resend-prompt: Prompt for the Resend API key and sender address for production verification emails.
exports.remote-cert-validator: Bootstrap openssl when needed, then verify existence, readability, expiry, cert/key match, and SAN coverage before deploy mutates the runtime TLS directory.
exports.edge-env-wiring: Generate DOMAIN, FRONTEND_URL, MTPROTO_BASE_DOMAIN, MTPROTO_BASE_SECRET_HEX, MTPROTO_SECRET_SALT, EMAIL_PROVIDER, RESEND_API_KEY, EMAIL_FROM, and EDGE_TLS_* values for the deployed .env.
exports.mtproto-runtime-env-wiring: Generate MTPROTO_RUNTIME_POLICY_URL, MTPROTO_RUNTIME_TOKEN, MTPROTO_POLICY_PORT, EDGE_SHARED_443_ENABLED, and EDGE_HTTPS_FALLBACK_PORT for the live edge runtime.
exports.phase38-de-edge-env-wiring: Plan DE runtime policy URL, RU SNI router targets, and cert reuse without printing token, private key, or proxy secrets.

operator-inputs.input-2: TLS_FULLCHAIN_PATH defaults to /root/krotpn-ssl/fullchain1.pem on the RU server.
operator-inputs.input-3: TLS_PRIVKEY_PATH defaults to /root/krotpn-ssl/privkey1.pem on the RU server.
operator-inputs.input-4: RESEND_API_KEY is entered by the operator and must never be echoed.

constraints.constraint-1: Production installs must not fall back to self-signed TLS unless the operator explicitly selects a dev/self-signed mode.
constraints.constraint-2: The private key must never be printed, copied into logs, embedded in XML evidence, or sent through unsafe command output.
constraints.constraint-3: Certificate validation must fail before docker compose changes when paths are missing, key mismatch occurs, SAN coverage is wrong, or the cert is expired.
constraints.constraint-4: Shared 443 MTProto routing remains a live-gated M-046/M-044 concern; this module only makes trusted wildcard TLS material deployable and reusable.
constraints.constraint-5: Operator certificate source paths must not be under /opt/KrotPN because installer refreshes that repository directory during clone.
constraints.constraint-6: Fresh deploys must generate non-empty 32-hex MTProto base secret and salt values without printing them.
constraints.constraint-7: Fresh production deploys must configure Resend email delivery or fail with an explicit operator message before registration can later hit provider_disabled.
constraints.constraint-8: Fresh deploys must generate an MTPROTO_RUNTIME_TOKEN and must not print it or reuse it outside the localhost policy bridge.
constraints.constraint-9: Hotfix deploys must preserve existing MTPROTO_BASE_SECRET_HEX and MTPROTO_SECRET_SALT so already-issued Telegram proxy links remain valid.
constraints.constraint-10: Phase-38 must not expose the DE policy API publicly and must not print wildcard private key material while preparing DE runtime TLS.

planned-execution.step-1: Extend install.sh prompt allowlist and interactive flow for domain, fullchain path, and privkey path after RU SSH credentials are known.
planned-execution.step-2: Pass sanitized TLS inputs to the remote deploy configuration without exposing private key contents.
planned-execution.step-3: Teach deploy/deploy-on-server.sh to validate and install operator-provided certs into /opt/KrotPN/ssl/server.crt and /opt/KrotPN/ssl/server.key.
planned-execution.step-4: Generate production domain and edge TLS env values for backend, frontend, admin, nginx, and MTProto base-domain expectations.
planned-execution.step-5: Preserve existing certs across redeploys unless a validated replacement is provided.
planned-execution.step-6: Collect and pass Resend API key and noreply@krotpn.xyz sender for production email verification delivery.
planned-execution.step-7: Generate and wire the live MTProto runtime policy token and shared-443 fallback env values.
planned-execution.step-8: Plan DE runtime TLS reuse, private policy URL, and RU SNI router env wiring for Phase-38.

evidence.evidence-1: 2026-05-17: Local Phase-35 implementation added installer prompts, RU cert preflight, deploy-time certificate validation/install, production env wiring, runtime nginx rendering, and static smoke coverage.
evidence.evidence-2: 2026-05-17: Live clean VPS deploy with prepared wildcard certificate files remains operator-required before STATUS can move beyond partial-local.
evidence.evidence-3: 2026-05-17: Reviewer follow-up fixed clean VPS openssl bootstrap risk, rejected /opt/KrotPN cert source paths, and added dynamic installer validator smoke checks.
evidence.evidence-4: 2026-05-17: Live clean VPS backend unhealthy failure was fixed by generating deploy-time MTProto secrets and adding smoke coverage against blank deploy values.
evidence.evidence-5: 2026-05-17: Phase-36 local execution added Resend secret prompt, sender validation, deploy env wiring, static smoke coverage, and redaction checks.
evidence.evidence-6: 2026-05-18: Phase-37 added deploy-time MTPROTO_RUNTIME_TOKEN generation, localhost policy URL wiring, shared-443 enablement, and static smoke guards for the mtproto-edge service.
evidence.evidence-7: 2026-05-18: Phase-38 plan requires DE runtime TLS/key handling and private policy URL wiring to stay redaction-safe before execution.

M-048.hotspot: H-034: Installer-provided wildcard TLS must validate cert/key/domain before deploy and must never log private key material.
